title: Warhammer Quest - Take a Look - Part 14 - Deathblow Issues 1-3 (complete)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oDeAAnpEEXU

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines all three issues of Deathblow, the official Warhammer Quest fanzine that mixed Games Workshop articles with fan-created content across a brief three-issue run.

Issue 1 is the meatiest, featuring "Downtown" rules for exploring settlements using dungeon tiles, a bank location for storing gold with interest, the "Eyes of Doom" adventure, official Lizardmen rules for the Old World, a Kislev expansion with yetis and ice elementals, and four new warrior types — though frustratingly incomplete, missing upgrade tables. Issue 2 reprints much of the White Dwarf content already covered but adds "The Shattered Amulet" campaign (loosely based on Advanced HeroQuest), critical wound tables called "Argh Me Spleen" for adding variety to combat, and three more partially complete heroes including the Dwarf Brewmaster.

Issue 3, the thinnest of all, introduces Arabian Nights as an exotic location, reprints more White Dwarf material, and includes an FAQ with rules clarifications by Andy Jones. Daniel notes that while collectors want these issues, the heavy reprinting and incomplete character rules make them less essential than the White Dwarf magazines themselves — though fan champion Littlemonk has since fleshed out the incomplete warriors on his website.

----

Do you prefer official-but-incomplete content, or fan-made material that's fully playable?
